title: Castanea roxburghii
taxonomy: Castanea roxburghii is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayas and southern China. Within the Fagaceae family, Castanea roxburghii is placed in the genus Castanea, which also includes other chestnut species. The specific classification of Castanea roxburghii is further supported by its unique morphological characteristics and geographic distribution. Scientifically, it is often referred to as Castanea roxburghii as its full binomial nomenclature.
uses: Castanea roxburghii is a plant species native to the Himalayan region and parts of China, and its seeds, also known as chilgoza, have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The health benefits of Castanea roxburghii include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to cardiovascular health and anti-aging.
